title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Five New England hotel restaurants made OpenTable's 2026 list of top 100 in America.
Massachusetts' Contessa and Mooo Beacon Hill, and Maine's Earth at Hidden Pond are on the list.
Michael Jordan's Steak House in Connecticut is highlighted for its upscale dining experience.

title: Revolution’s Marko Mitrović reflected on World Cup memories
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Marko Mitrović shares childhood memories of watching the World Cup from Belgrade, starting with the 1982 tournament.
Mitrović recounts the 1990 World Cup excitement overshadowing his aunt's wedding due to Yugoslavia's match against Argentina.
As New England Revolution coach, Mitrović reflects on representing Serbia in youth soccer and coaching US teams in recent tournaments.

title: Joe Mazzulla showcased MMA training regimen, spoke about its influence on coaching the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Joe Mazzulla trains in Brazilian jiu-jitsu with Eric Nicksick, contributing to his fitness and coaching strategies.
Mazzulla explores the psychological aspects of MMA, relating it to his coaching communication techniques with the Celtics.
Mazzulla plans to continue MMA training, aiming to use the mentality to enhance the team's performance.

title: Report: Wrong-way driver who killed trooper had 9 drinks before Route 1 crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Wrong-way driver Hernan Ramon Marrero had a blood alcohol level of 0.192, twice the legal limit, after drinking nine alcoholic beverages.
Trooper Kevin Trainor, 30, died following a head-on collision with Marrero on Route 1, returning from a public safety detail.
Traffic footage showed Marrero bypassed 'WRONG WAY' signs, drove 1.8 miles southbound in a northbound lane, causing the fatal crash.

title: The best state to move to in 2026 is in New England — and it’s not Massachusetts
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: New Hampshire ranked No. 1 as the best state to move to in 2026, based on affordability, safety, and economic strength.
Maine ranked fifth, noted for its low violent crime rate and high air quality, contributing to its safety and quality of life.
Massachusetts, ranked sixth, excelled in healthcare and education but lagged in affordability and economic strength.

title: Acclaimed Seaport restaurant Chickadee to close after 8 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Chickadee, a James Beard-nominated restaurant in Boston's Seaport, is set to close on July 18 after eight years.
Opened by Chef John DaSilva and Ted Kilpatrick in 2018, Chickadee gained fame for its Mediterranean-inspired dishes.
The restaurant navigated the pandemic with a strong takeout program and hosted a popular monthly Cookbook Club series.

title: Man charged with boating under the influence after Mass. college student drowns in Connecticut
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Michael Collins charged with boating under the influence after a canoe accident in Connecticut resulted in a drowning.
Dominick Tocci, a Holy Cross student, drowned after the canoe he was in overturned on Lake Hayward.
Collins was arrested on July 10 and is scheduled to appear in court on July 23.

title: Nine years after South End killing, fugitive wanted on murder charge captured in North Carolina
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Alan Marcus Lewis, wanted for a 2017 Boston murder, was captured by U.S. Marshals in Charlotte, North Carolina.
Lewis is charged in the fatal shooting of Brent Stevenson in Boston's South End on November 6, 2017.
After years on the run, Lewis was found living under assumed identities in multiple locations, including Texas and North Carolina.

title: The people keeping Cape Cod’s car culture alive
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cape Cod Classics hosts over 20 events annually, bringing together nearly 500 car enthusiasts and raising over $11,000 for charities.
British car enthusiasts gather at Cape Cod's Lemon Tree Shops, showcasing classic models and engaging in club-hosted charity events.
Heritage Museums & Gardens volunteers restored 32 out of 43 antique cars, preserving their historical operation and appearance.

title: Amtrak warns customers that trains may experience delays this week due to ‘extreme temperatures’
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Amtrak warns of potential train delays due to extreme heat in the Northeast from July 15 to 17.
Temperatures in Boston reached the 90s, prompting Mayor Michelle Wu to issue a heat advisory.
Amtrak reduces train speeds when track temperatures exceed 128 degrees as a safety measure.
